🍦 SwiftServe
Menu Scoop Agents About
Get

Menu / on watchOS

The state of watchOS

What the Swift ecosystem actually serves on watchOS — every count below is derived from the records, and every verdict carries its receipt.

104serve it
12fenced out — proven
85honest unknowns

across 201 records · 107 packages — counted at generation time, never by hand

Built into the OS

8 Apple frameworks already cover 26 capabilities on watchOS — nothing to add to your Package.swift. Floors come from Apple's own symbol graphs.

AVFAudio built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Echo cancellation
  • Audio file reading/writing
  • Audio level metering
  • Audio playback
  • Microphone recording
  • Audio session management
  • Audio synthesis / DSP

AVFoundation built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • HLS streaming
  • Video playback

CloudKit built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Cloud sync

CoreData built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Cloud sync
  • Object / document store
  • Database encryption
  • In-memory database
  • Schema migrations
  • Reactive queries / change tracking
  • Type-safe queries

MediaPlayer built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Now-playing / remote controls

Security built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Keychain storage

SoundAnalysis built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Sound classification

SwiftData built in

as of Xcode 26.6

  • Cloud sync
  • Object / document store
  • In-memory database
  • Schema migrations
  • Reactive queries / change tracking
  • Type-safe queries

Packages by capability

How many verified packages serve each capability on watchOS. Every link opens the truth table focused on watchOS — near-misses included.

Audio

  • Background audio playback 0 of 1 package
  • Sound classification 0 of 1 package in the OS
  • MP3 encoding/decoding 0 of 1 package
  • Opus encoding/decoding 1 of 1 package
  • Audio device selection 0 of 2 packages
  • Echo cancellation 0 of 1 package in the OS
  • Audio file reading/writing 0 of 1 package in the OS
  • Audio level metering 0 of 1 package in the OS
  • Noise cancellation 0 of 2 packages
  • Pitch detection 0 of 1 package
  • Audio playback 0 of 5 packages in the OS
  • Microphone recording 0 of 3 packages in the OS
  • Audio session management 0 of 2 packages in the OS
  • Spatial audio no packages needed
  • Audio streaming 0 of 3 packages
  • Audio synthesis / DSP 0 of 6 packages in the OS
  • Waveform visualization 0 of 4 packages

Images

  • Async image loading 3 of 4 packages
  • Image caching 2 of 2 packages
  • Downsampling / resizing 1 of 1 package
  • Animated GIF playback 0 of 3 packages
  • Prefetching 2 of 2 packages
  • SVG rendering 1 of 1 package
  • WebP decoding 0 of 1 package

Media

  • Now-playing / remote controls 0 of 1 package in the OS
  • HLS streaming 1 of 3 packages in the OS
  • RTMP streaming 1 of 1 package
  • WebRTC real-time streaming 0 of 3 packages

MIDI

  • MIDI file read/write 1 of 3 packages
  • MIDI input/output 0 of 5 packages

Networking

  • Certificate pinning 1 of 1 package
  • HTTP client 2 of 2 packages
  • Network logging / inspection 1 of 1 package
  • Multipart upload 1 of 1 package
  • Network reachability 0 of 1 package
  • Request stubbing / mocking 1 of 1 package
  • Request retry / backoff 1 of 1 package
  • Server-sent events 1 of 1 package
  • WebSockets 2 of 2 packages

Speech

  • Speech-to-text 1 of 5 packages
  • Text-to-speech 1 of 6 packages

Storage

  • Cloud sync 1 of 1 package in the OS
  • Codable persistence 6 of 6 packages
  • Concurrent access 3 of 3 packages
  • Disk caching 2 of 2 packages
  • Object / document store 1 of 1 package in the OS
  • Database encryption 0 of 3 packages in the OS
  • Full-text search 2 of 2 packages
  • In-memory database 5 of 6 packages in the OS
  • Key-value storage 3 of 3 packages
  • Keychain storage 3 of 3 packages in the OS
  • Schema migrations 5 of 5 packages in the OS
  • Reactive queries / change tracking 6 of 6 packages in the OS
  • Type-safe queries 5 of 5 packages in the OS
  • SQLite access 4 of 4 packages

Ui

  • Auto Layout DSL 0 of 1 package
  • Bottom sheets 0 of 2 packages
  • Charts 0 of 1 package
  • Haptic feedback 1 of 2 packages
  • Markdown rendering 1 of 1 package
  • Paging / carousel 1 of 1 package
  • Particle effects 2 of 3 packages
  • Loading indicators 0 of 2 packages
  • Pull to refresh 0 of 1 package
  • Rich text editing 0 of 1 package
  • Skeleton loading 1 of 1 package
  • Toasts / banners 1 of 3 packages
  • View transitions / motion effects 0 of 1 package
  • Vector animation playback 0 of 1 package

Video

  • Video calling 0 of 2 packages
  • Camera capture 0 of 2 packages
  • Video playback 0 of 6 packages in the OS
  • Screen sharing 0 of 2 packages

Voice

  • Voice calling 0 of 2 packages

The fence list

12 verdicts across 12 packages, proven off watchOS. Where a build verdict grounds the claim, the receipt is the compiler's own words at the pinned commit — and the same commit builds elsewhere, so the fence is real, not toolchain rot.

Alamofire as of 5.12.0

Network reachability serves it on iOSmacOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

#if canImport(SystemConfiguration) — fenced canImport(SystemConfiguration) — the framework watchOS doesn't have

AVFAudio as of Xcode 26.6

Spatial audio serves it on iOSmacOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

not exported by the watchOS SDK symbol graph

charts as of 5.1.0

Charts serves it on iOSmacOStvOSCatalyst

#if os(iOS) || os(tvOS) — Core NSUIView shim (base class of every chart view) exists only under os(iOS) || os(tvOS); surface digest marks watchOS absent.

CoreHaptics as of Xcode 26.6

Haptic feedback serves it on iOSmacOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

absent from the watchOS SDK symbol graphs

CoreMIDI as of Xcode 26.6

MIDI input/output serves it on iOSmacOSvisionOSCatalyst

not exported by the watchOS SDK symbol graph

drops as of 1.7.0

Toasts / banners serves it on iOSCatalyst

#if os(iOS) — Core Drops class is guarded by #if os(iOS); surface digest resolves watchOS as absent (gap) for this decl.

Kingfisher as of 8.10.0

Animated GIF playback serves it on iOSmacOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

#if !os(watchOS) — fenced !os(watchOS)

LiveKit as of 2.15.1

Audio session management serves it on iOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

#if os(iOS) || os(visionOS) || os(tvOS)

MIDIKitIO as of 1.1.0

MIDI input/output serves it on iOSmacOSvisionOSCatalyst

#if !os(tvOS) && !os(watchOS) — MIDI endpoint object — the I/O surface

pow as of 1.0.6

Haptic feedback serves it on iOSCatalyst

#if os(iOS) — Core haptics symbol is guarded by #if os(iOS); surface digest gaps mark watchOS absent. Pow provides no watchOS haptics path (WatchKit haptics are not wrapped).

SDWebImageSwiftUI as of 3.1.4

Animated GIF playback serves it on iOSmacOStvOSvisionOSCatalyst

#if !os(watchOS) — fenced !os(watchOS)

SwiftySound as of 1.3.0

Audio session management serves it on iOStvOSCatalyst

#if os(iOS) || os(tvOS) — session categories exist only where AVAudioSession does

Honest unknowns

85 verdicts we can't call yet — and refuse to guess. Each carries the reason on record.

  • AECAudioStream — Echo cancellation

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

  • AudioKit — Audio file reading/writing

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioKit — Audio level metering

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioKit — Microphone recording

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence proves absence

  • AudioKit — MIDI file read/write

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioKit — MIDI input/output

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; MIDI symbols resolve present but intent is unstated

  • AudioKit Waveform — Waveform visualization

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioKitUI — Waveform visualization

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioManager — Audio playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • AudioRecorder — Microphone recording

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • CAAudioHardware — Audio device selection

    manifest declares macOS only

  • ChunkedAudioPlayer — Audio streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • CombineMIDI — MIDI input/output

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • DevoloopAudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • DSWaveformImage — Waveform visualization

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • DunneAudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • ExyteMediaPicker — Camera capture

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • FDSoundActivatedRecorder — Microphone recording

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • FDWaveformView — Waveform visualization

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • floatingpanel — Bottom sheets

    Manifest declares only .iOS(.v12); no watchOS floor. UIKit-based UIViewController surface; digest records no watchOS gap but no positive build evidence either.

  • FluidAudio — Speech-to-text

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • FRadioPlayer — Audio streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • Gifu — Animated GIF playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • grdb.swift — Database encryption

    no claim recorded for this platform yet

  • grdb.swift — In-memory database

    README: 'Database queues support in-memory databases' (v7.11.1 README.md line 423)

  • HLSCore — HLS streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • HLSVideoCache — HLS streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • Kingfisher — WebP decoding

    WebP needs the separate KingfisherWebP coder package — nothing in this package serves it

  • KSPlayer — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • LiveKit — Noise cancellation

    watchOS absent from the manifest platforms (iOS/macOS/macCatalyst/tvOS); the SDK's WebRTC binary dependency makes watchOS support doubtful, but nothing in source proves absence

  • LiveKit — WebRTC real-time streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • LiveKit — Video calling

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • LiveKit — Screen sharing

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • LiveKit — Voice calling

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • LiveKitKrispNoiseFilter — Noise cancellation

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • lottie-ios — Vector animation playback

    Package.swift declares no watchOS platform floor (iOS/macOS/tvOS/visionOS only). Missing floor is not absence evidence, so status is unknown.

  • MidiParser — MIDI file read/write

    empty manifest

  • MLXAudio — Text-to-speech

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • ModernAVPlayer — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

  • MorkAndMIDI — MIDI input/output

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • MuxPlayerSwift — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

  • Player — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

  • popupview — Bottom sheets

    Public decl present per surface digest, but internal sheet implementation is os(iOS)-gated; behavior on watchOS unverified

  • pow — Particle effects

    Surface shows no watchOS gap (decl is unguarded), but gaps are #if-derived, not build verdicts; manifest declares no watchOS floor and README requirements omit watchOS. No build verification, so unknown.

  • pow — View transitions / motion effects

    Surface shows no watchOS gap (decl is unguarded), but gaps are #if-derived, not build verdicts; manifest declares no watchOS floor and README requirements omit watchOS. No build verification, so unknown.

  • progresshud — Loading indicators

    Only .iOS(.v17) declared; missing floor is not absence evidence.

  • progresshud — Toasts / banners

    Only .iOS(.v17) declared; missing floor is not absence evidence.

  • realm-swift — Database encryption

    README 'Fully Encrypted' section demonstrates Realm.Configuration(encryptionKey: key) with a 64-byte key ('Data can be encrypted in-flight and at-rest').

  • richtextkit — Rich text editing

    The core editor guard omits watchOS (with 'iOS'/'macOS' defined only for iOS/macCatalyst/macOS in Package.swift), so the editor view is compiled out on watchOS. The digest resolves this as conditional, not absent, so absence cannot be proven.

  • SDWebImage — Async image loading

    SDWebImage's surface is Objective-C (214 files unparsed in v1) — honestly unverified rather than guessed; manifest floors: iOS 9 / macOS 10.11 era

  • SFBAudioEngine — Audio playback

    the playback engine's primary surface is Objective-C (194 files unparsed in v1); Swift-side extraction found no anchorable player symbol — honestly unverified rather than guessed

  • snapkit — Auto Layout DSL

    UIView-backed DSL branch: digest gaps mark watchOS absent for this variant.

  • SoulAudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SoundpipeAudioKit — Pitch detection

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SpeziSpeech — Speech-to-text

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SpeziSpeech — Text-to-speech

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SPFKAudioHardware — Audio device selection

    manifest declares macOS only

  • SPFKSoundAnalysis — Sound classification

    manifest declares macOS only

  • SporthAudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • sqlite.swift — Database encryption

    SQLCipher product is linked only .when(platforms: applePlatforms, traits: ["SQLCipher"]); watchOS is in applePlatforms, so encryption works when the SQLCipher trait is enabled — conditional, hence unknown.

  • STKAudioKit — Audio synthesis / DSP

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • StreamVideo — WebRTC real-time streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors (iOS-only manifest)

  • StreamVideo — Video calling

    watchOS absent from manifest floors (iOS-only manifest)

  • StreamVideo — Screen sharing

    watchOS absent from manifest floors (iOS-only manifest)

  • StreamVideo — Voice calling

    watchOS absent from manifest floors (iOS-only manifest)

  • swift-system-sound — Audio playback

    empty manifest

  • SwiftAudioEx — Background audio playback

    manifest declares iOS only

  • SwiftAudioEx — Audio playback

    manifest declares iOS only; AVPlayer exists elsewhere but the package states no intent

  • SwiftAudioEx — Now-playing / remote controls

    manifest declares iOS only; AVPlayer exists elsewhere but the package states no intent

  • SwiftAudioPlayer — Audio streaming

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SwiftEdgeTTS — Text-to-speech

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SwiftLAME — MP3 encoding/decoding

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SwiftSpeech — Speech-to-text

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • SwiftTTS — Text-to-speech

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • swiftui-pull-to-refresh — Loading indicators

    Surface parses PRESENT, but UIViewRepresentable/UIActivityIndicatorView are unavailable on watchOS and the reference is unguarded; unknown.

  • swiftui-pull-to-refresh — Pull to refresh

    Surface parses PRESENT, but UIViewRepresentable/UIActivityIndicatorView are unavailable on watchOS and the reference is unguarded; no absence guard evidence, so unknown.

  • SwiftWhisper — Speech-to-text

    memory constraints make whisper.cpp doubtful; nothing proven

  • Swifty360Player — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

  • SwiftySound — Audio playback

    README targets iOS/macOS/tvOS; no watchOS signal either way

  • VideoIO — Camera capture

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no camera API there anyway — but nothing in source proves it

  • VOICEVOX — Text-to-speech

    watchOS absent from manifest floors

  • WebMIDIKit — MIDI input/output

    empty manifest

  • WebRTC (stasel) — WebRTC real-time streaming

    binary-only

  • YouTubePlayerKit — Video playback

    watchOS absent from manifest floors; no source fence

This whole page, one fetch: /api/on/watchos.json — counts, coverage, fences, and receipts as static JSON.

Served fresh · 74 capabilities × 107 packages · 201 records · data v1

Every verdict links to the exact source line that decides it. How it works

Made refreshingly cool from Paris, France
